intro: Poacynum hendersonii, also known as the Hawaiian milkweed vine, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Poacynum hendersonii include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antiviral, and anticancer activities.
